metadata.methodology: Judicial diversity figures are sourced from the MOJ's annual Diversity of the Judiciary statistical bulletin, which counts judges in post at 1 April each year. Ethnicity data relies on self-declaration and non-declaration rates of 10-15% introduce uncertainty. Gender is recorded as binary (male/female) in official statistics. Court level breakdowns are for courts in England and Wales only.
knownIssues: Non-declaration rates for ethnicity are 10-15% across the judiciary, meaning ethnic minority representation is likely underestimated
Statistics do not capture socioeconomic background — the dominant axis of judicial homogeneity
Magistrates are included in overall figures but have a very different profile to salaried judges, which can skew aggregates
Disability data has a non-declaration rate above 30% and is not reliably reported
